docs.title: Nexa Standard Library Reference
docs.text: This document describes all namespaces, tool functions, parameters, and usage examples of the Nexa standard library (std).

docs.title: 📋 Overview
docs.text: The Nexa standard library provides built-in tools that enable Agents to interact with the external world. After declaring usage permissions via the uses keyword, Agents can call these tools.

docs.title: Namespace Overview
docs.text: Namespace Description Tool Count std.fs File system operations 6 std.http HTTP network requests 4 std.time Time and date operations 5 std.json JSON data processing 3 std.text Text processing 4 std.hash Encryption and encoding 4 std.math Mathematical operations 2 std.regex Regular expressions 2 std.shell Shell command execution 2 std.ask_human Human interaction 1

docs.title: 📁 std.fs - File System Operations
docs.text: File system operations are fundamental capabilities for agents to interact with the local environment.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters file_read Read file content path file_write Write to file path, content file_append Append to file path, content file_exists Check if file exists path file_list List directory files directory file_delete Delete file path

docs.title: file_read
docs.text:
Read file content.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description path string Yes - File path encoding string No utf-8 Encoding format

Return Value: File content string, or error message on failure.

Example:

agent FileReader uses std.fs {
    role: "File reading assistant",
    prompt: "Help users read file content"
}

flow main {
    content = FileReader.run("Read file /tmp/data.txt");
    print(content);
}

docs.title: file_write
docs.text:
Write content to file. Creates directory if it doesn't exist.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description path string Yes - File path content string Yes - File content encoding string No utf-8 Encoding format

Return Value: Success message with character count.

Example:

agent FileWriter uses std.fs {
    role: "File writing assistant",
    prompt: "Help users write files"
}

flow main {
    result = FileWriter.run("Write 'Hello Nexa' to /tmp/output.txt");
    print(result);
}

docs.title: file_append
docs.text:
Append content to end of file.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description path string Yes - File path content string Yes - Content to append encoding string No utf-8 Encoding format

Return Value: Success message with appended character count.

Example:

agent LogWriter uses std.fs {
    role: "Log recording assistant",
    prompt: "Help users append logs"
}

flow main {
    LogWriter.run("Append '[2024-01-01] New log entry' to /var/log/app.log");
}

docs.title: file_exists
docs.text:
Check if file or directory exists.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description path string Yes - File/directory path

Return Value: JSON format { "exists": bool, "path": string }.

Example:

agent FileChecker uses std.fs {
    role: "File checking assistant",
    prompt: "Check if file exists"
}

flow main {
    result = FileChecker.run("Check if /tmp/config.json exists");
    print(result);  // {"exists": true, "path": "/tmp/config.json"}
}

docs.title: file_list
docs.text:
List files in directory.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description directory string Yes - Directory path pattern string No * File match pattern (glob format)

Return Value: JSON format { "files": [string], "count": int }.

Example:

agent DirectoryScanner uses std.fs {
    role: "Directory scanning assistant",
    prompt: "List files in directory"
}

flow main {
    // List all files
    all_files = DirectoryScanner.run("List all files in /tmp directory");

    // List only .txt files
    txt_files = DirectoryScanner.run("List *.txt files in /tmp directory");
    print(txt_files);
}

docs.title: file_delete
docs.text:
Delete file.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description path string Yes - File path

Return Value: Success or error message.

Example:

agent FileCleaner uses std.fs {
    role: "File cleanup assistant",
    prompt: "Delete specified files"
}

flow main {
    result = FileCleaner.run("Delete /tmp/old_data.txt");
    print(result);
}

docs.title: 🌐 std.http - HTTP Network Requests
docs.text: Native network request capabilities supporting RESTful API calls.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters http_get GET request url http_post POST request url, data http_put PUT request url, data http_delete DELETE request url

docs.title: http_get
docs.text:
Send HTTP GET request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description url string Yes - Request URL headers object No {} Request headers dictionary timeout int No 30 Timeout in seconds

Return Value: Response content string.

Example:

agent WebFetcher uses std.http {
    role: "Web fetching assistant",
    prompt: "Fetch web content"
}

flow main {
    content = WebFetcher.run("GET https://api.example.com/data");
    print(content);
}

docs.title: http_post
docs.text:
Send HTTP POST request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description url string Yes - Request URL data string Yes - Request body content headers object No {} Request headers dictionary content_type string No application/json Content type timeout int No 30 Timeout in seconds

Return Value: JSON format { "status": int, "body": string }.

Example:

agent ApiClient uses std.http {
    role: "API client",
    prompt: "Call RESTful API"
}

flow main {
    payload = '{"name": "Nexa", "version": "1.0"}';
    result = ApiClient.run(f"POST {payload} to https://api.example.com/create");
    print(result);
}

docs.title: http_put
docs.text:
Send HTTP PUT request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description url string Yes - Request URL data string Yes - Request body content headers object No {} Request headers dictionary timeout int No 30 Timeout in seconds

Return Value: JSON format { "status": int, "body": string }.

Example:

agent DataUpdater uses std.http {
    role: "Data update assistant",
    prompt: "Update remote data"
}

flow main {
    update_data = '{"status": "completed"}';
    result = DataUpdater.run(f"PUT {update_data} to https://api.example.com/item/123");
}

docs.title: http_delete
docs.text:
Send HTTP DELETE request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description url string Yes - Request URL headers object No {} Request headers dictionary timeout int No 30 Timeout in seconds

Return Value: JSON format { "status": int, "body": string }.

Example:

agent ResourceDeleter uses std.http {
    role: "Resource deletion assistant",
    prompt: "Delete remote resources"
}

flow main {
    result = ResourceDeleter.run("DELETE https://api.example.com/item/123");
}

docs.title: 🕐 std.time - Time and Date Operations
docs.text: Time-related operation tools.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters time_now Get current time None time_diff Calculate time difference start, end time_format Format time iso_string time_sleep Sleep for specified seconds seconds time_timestamp Get timestamp None

docs.title: time_now
docs.text:
Get current time.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description format string No %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S Time format

Return Value: Formatted time string.

Format Specifiers:

Format Description Example %Y Year 2024 %m Month 01-12 %d Day 01-31 %H Hour (24-hour) 00-23 %M Minute 00-59 %S Second 00-59

Example:

agent TimeHelper uses std.time {
    role: "Time assistant",
    prompt: "Provide time-related services"
}

flow main {
    // Default format
    now = TimeHelper.run("Get current time");
    print(now);  // 2024-01-15 10:30:00

    // Custom format
    date_only = TimeHelper.run("Get current date in format %Y-%m-%d");
    print(date_only);  // 2024-01-15
}

docs.title: time_diff
docs.text:
Calculate difference between two times.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description start string Yes - Start time (ISO format) end string Yes - End time (ISO format) unit string No seconds Unit: seconds/minutes/hours/days

Return Value: JSON format { "value": float, "unit": string, "days": int, "seconds": float }.

Example:

agent DurationCalculator uses std.time {
    role: "Duration calculator",
    prompt: "Calculate time difference"
}

flow main {
    result = DurationCalculator.run(
        "Calculate hours between 2024-01-01T00:00:00 and 2024-01-02T12:00:00"
    );
    print(result);  // {"value": 36, "unit": "hours", ...}
}

docs.title: time_format
docs.text:
Convert ISO format time to specified format.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description iso_string string Yes - ISO time string format string No %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S Output format

Return Value: Formatted time string.

Example:

agent DateFormatter uses std.time {
    role: "Date formatting assistant",
    prompt: "Convert time format"
}

flow main {
    result = DateFormatter.run(
        "Format 2024-01-15T10:30:00 to show only date"
    );
    print(result);  // 2024-01-15
}

docs.title: time_sleep
docs.text:
Pause execution for specified seconds.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description seconds int Yes - Sleep seconds

Return Value: JSON format { "sleep": int }.

Example:

agent DelayedTask uses std.time {
    role: "Delayed task assistant",
    prompt: "Execute delayed tasks"
}

flow main {
    print("Starting wait...");
    DelayedTask.run("Wait for 5 seconds");
    print("Wait complete!");
}

docs.title: time_timestamp
docs.text:
Get current Unix timestamp.

Parameters: None

Return Value: JSON format { "timestamp": int }.

Example:

agent TimestampGenerator uses std.time {
    role: "Timestamp generator",
    prompt: "Generate timestamps"
}

flow main {
    ts = TimestampGenerator.run("Get current timestamp");
    print(ts);  // {"timestamp": 1705312200}
}

docs.title: 📦 std.json - JSON Data Processing
docs.text: JSON data parsing, querying, and serialization.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters json_parse Parse JSON text json_get Get JSON path value text, path json_stringify Serialize to JSON data

docs.title: json_parse
docs.text:
Parse JSON string.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - JSON string

Return Value: Formatted JSON string (easy to read).

Example:

agent JsonParser uses std.json {
    role: "JSON parsing assistant",
    prompt: "Parse JSON data"
}

flow main {
    raw = '{"name":"Nexa","version":1.0}';
    parsed = JsonParser.run(f"Parse {raw}");
    print(parsed);
}

docs.title: json_get
docs.text:
Extract value from specified JSON path.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - JSON string path string Yes - Path (e.g., data.items.0)

Return Value: Value at the specified path.

Path Format: - Use . to separate levels - Array indices use numbers, e.g., items.0.name

Example:

agent DataExtractor uses std.json {
    role: "Data extraction assistant",
    prompt: "Extract data from JSON"
}

flow main {
    json_data = '{"user":{"name":"Alice","age":30}}';
    name = DataExtractor.run(f"Get user.name from {json_data}");
    print(name);  // "Alice"
}

docs.title: json_stringify
docs.text:
Serialize data to JSON string.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description data string Yes - Data object (JSON format string) indent int No 2 Indent spaces

Return Value: Formatted JSON string.

Example:

agent JsonSerializer uses std.json {
    role: "JSON serialization assistant",
    prompt: "Serialize data to JSON"
}

flow main {
    data = '{"name":"Nexa"}';
    result = JsonSerializer.run(f"Serialize {data} with 4-space indent");
    print(result);
}

docs.title: 📝 std.text - Text Processing
docs.text: Text processing tools.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters text_split Split text text text_replace Replace text text, old, new text_upper Convert to uppercase text text_lower Convert to lowercase text

docs.title: text_split
docs.text:
Split text into parts.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Text to split delimiter string No \n Delimiter max_splits int No -1 Maximum splits (-1 means unlimited)

Return Value: JSON format { "parts": [string], "count": int }.

Example:

agent TextSplitter uses std.text {
    role: "Text splitting assistant",
    prompt: "Split text"
}

flow main {
    text = "one,two,three,four";
    result = TextSplitter.run(f"Split {text} by comma");
    print(result);  // {"parts": ["one", "two", "three", "four"], "count": 4}
}

docs.title: text_replace
docs.text:
Replace content in text.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Original text old string Yes - Content to replace new string Yes - Replacement content count int No -1 Replace count (-1 means all)

Return Value: Replaced text.

Example:

agent TextReplacer uses std.text {
    role: "Text replacement assistant",
    prompt: "Replace content in text"
}

flow main {
    text = "Hello World, Hello Nexa";
    result = TextReplacer.run(f"Replace Hello with Hi in {text}");
    print(result);  // "Hi World, Hi Nexa"
}

docs.title: text_upper
docs.text:
Convert text to uppercase.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Input text

Return Value: Uppercase text.

docs.title: text_lower
docs.text:
Convert text to lowercase.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Input text

Return Value: Lowercase text.

docs.title: 🔐 std.hash - Encryption and Encoding
docs.text: Hash calculation and encoding tools.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters hash_md5 MD5 hash text hash_sha256 SHA256 hash text base64_encode Base64 encode text base64_decode Base64 decode text

docs.title: hash_md5
docs.text:
Calculate MD5 hash of text.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Input text

Return Value: 32-character MD5 hash string.

Example:

agent HashHelper uses std.hash {
    role: "Hash calculation assistant",
    prompt: "Calculate text hash"
}

flow main {
    hash = HashHelper.run("Calculate MD5 of 'Hello Nexa'");
    print(hash);  // 32-character hash value
}

docs.title: hash_sha256
docs.text:
Calculate SHA256 hash of text.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Input text

Return Value: 64-character SHA256 hash string.

docs.title: base64_encode
docs.text:
Encode text to Base64 format.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Input text

Return Value: Base64 encoded string.

docs.title: base64_decode
docs.text:
Decode Base64 text.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description text string Yes - Base64 encoded text

Return Value: Decoded original text.

docs.title: 🔢 std.math - Mathematical Operations
docs.text: Mathematical calculation tools.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters math_calc Calculate math expression expression math_random Generate random number min_val, max_val

docs.title: math_calc
docs.text:
Safely calculate mathematical expression.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description expression string Yes - Math expression

Return Value: JSON format { "result": number, "expression": string }.

Security Limitation

Only allows digits and +-*/.() characters, other characters are rejected.

Example:

agent Calculator uses std.math {
    role: "Math calculation assistant",
    prompt: "Calculate math expressions"
}

flow main {
    result = Calculator.run("Calculate (10 + 5) * 2");
    print(result);  // {"result": 30, "expression": "(10 + 5) * 2"}
}

docs.title: math_random
docs.text:
Generate random integer within specified range.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description min_val int Yes - Minimum value max_val int Yes - Maximum value

Return Value: Random integer.

Example:

agent RandomGenerator uses std.math {
    role: "Random number generator",
    prompt: "Generate random numbers"
}

flow main {
    num = RandomGenerator.run("Generate random number between 1 and 100");
    print(num);
}

docs.title: 🔍 std.regex - Regular Expressions
docs.text: Regular expression matching and replacement.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters regex_match Regex match pattern, text regex_replace Regex replace pattern, replacement, text

docs.title: regex_match
docs.text:
Match text using regular expression.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description pattern string Yes - Regex pattern text string Yes - Text to match flags string No "" Flags: i(ignore case), m(multiline), s(singleline)

Return Value: JSON format { "matches": [string], "count": int }.

Example:

agent PatternMatcher uses std.regex {
    role: "Pattern matching assistant",
    prompt: "Match regex patterns"
}

flow main {
    text = "Email: test@example.com, Phone: 123-456-7890";
    emails = PatternMatcher.run(f"Match all email addresses in {text}");
    print(emails);
}

docs.title: regex_replace
docs.text:
Replace text using regular expression.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description pattern string Yes - Regex pattern replacement string Yes - Replacement content text string Yes - Text to process flags string No "" Flags

Return Value: Replaced text.

docs.title: 💻 std.shell - Shell Commands
docs.text: Execute system shell commands.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters shell_exec Execute command command shell_which Find command path command

docs.title: shell_exec
docs.text:
Execute shell command.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description command string Yes - Command to execute timeout int No 30 Timeout in seconds

Return Value: JSON format { "stdout": string, "stderr": string, "returncode": int, "success": bool }.

Security Warning

Shell command execution has potential risks. Please ensure: - Don't execute dangerous commands (like rm -rf /) - Validate input parameters - Set reasonable timeout

Example:

agent ShellRunner uses std.shell {
    role: "Shell execution assistant",
    prompt: "Execute system commands"
}

flow main {
    result = ShellRunner.run("Execute ls -la /tmp");
    print(result.stdout);
}

docs.title: shell_which
docs.text:
Find executable file path for command.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description command string Yes - Command name

Return Value: JSON format { "command": string, "path": string, "found": bool }.

docs.title: 🙋 std.ask_human - Human Interaction
docs.text: Human-in-the-Loop interaction tool.

docs.title: Tool List
docs.text: Tool Description Required Parameters ask_human Request user input prompt

docs.title: ask_human
docs.text:
Request user input for Human-in-the-Loop interaction.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Default Description prompt string Yes - Prompt message default string No "" Default value (used when user doesn't input)

Return Value: User input string.

Example:

agent HumanInterface uses std.ask_human {
    role: "Human interaction assistant",
    prompt: "Interact with user for confirmation"
}

flow main {
    // Wait for user input
    confirmation = HumanInterface.run("Please confirm if to continue? [y/n]");

    if (confirmation == "y") {
        print("User confirmed, continuing...");
    } else {
        print("User rejected, stopping");
    }
}

docs.title: Declare Usage Permission
docs.text:
Use uses keyword in Agent definition:

// Single namespace
agent MyAgent uses std.fs {
    prompt: "..."
}

// Multiple namespaces
agent MultiToolAgent uses std.fs, std.http, std.time {
    prompt: "..."
}

docs.title: Runtime Invocation
docs.text:
Agents call tools via natural language instructions:

agent Assistant uses std.fs, std.http {
    role: "Multi-function assistant",
    prompt: "Help users complete file and network operations"
}

flow main {
    // Natural language tool invocation
    result = Assistant.run("Read /tmp/data.txt and POST to https://api.example.com/upload");
    print(result);
}

docs.title: Log Management Agent
docs.text:
agent LogManager uses std.fs, std.time {
    role: "Log management expert",
    model: "deepseek/deepseek-chat",
    prompt: """
    You are a log management expert, you can:
    - Read and analyze log files
    - Create and append log entries
    - Provide timestamp information
    """
}

flow main {
    task = """
    1. Get current timestamp
    2. Create log entry: [timestamp] System started
    3. Append to /var/log/nexa.log
    """;

    result = LogManager.run(task);
    print(result);
}

docs.title: API Data Fetcher Agent
docs.text:
agent DataFetcher uses std.http, std.json {
    role: "Data fetching expert",
    prompt: "Fetch data from API and parse JSON"
}

flow main {
    task = """
    1. GET https://api.example.com/users
    2. Parse returned JSON
    3. Extract name field of first user
    """;

    name = DataFetcher.run(task);
    print(f"First user: {name}");
}

docs.title: 📊 Version History
docs.text:
Version Updates v1.0 Added std.text, std.hash, std.math, std.regex namespaces v0.9.7 Added std.shell, std.ask_human namespaces v0.9 Completed file operation tools (append, delete) v0.8 Added HTTP PUT/DELETE methods v0.7 Initial standard library release (std.fs, std.http, std.time)

Last updated: 2026-03-28 | Based on Nexa v0.9.7-alpha / v1.0-alpha AVM

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: This document summarizes common problems and their solutions when using Nexa. If you encounter a problem, please check this guide first.

docs.title: 📋 Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Installation and Environment Issues
  • Syntax Error Troubleshooting
  • Runtime Error Troubleshooting
  • Model Call Issues
  • Tool Call Issues
  • Protocol Related Issues
  • Debugging Tips

docs.title: 1.1 pip install Failed
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement nexa

Reason: Nexa has not been published to PyPI yet, need to install from source.

Solution: 

git clone https://github.com/your-org/nexa.git
cd nexa
pip install -e .

docs.title: 1.2 Python Version Incompatible
docs.text:
Symptom: 

SyntaxError: invalid syntax
or 
TypeError: ... got an unexpected keyword argument

Reason: Nexa requires Python 3.10 or higher.

Solution: 

# Check Python version
python --version

# If version is lower than 3.10, upgrade or use virtual environment
# Using conda
conda create -n nexa python=3.10
conda activate nexa

# Or using venv
python3.10 -m venv nexa-env
source nexa-env/bin/activate  # Linux/macOS
# nexa-env\Scripts\activate   # Windows

docs.title: 1.3 Dependency Conflicts
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ERROR: Cannot install nexa because these package versions have conflicting dependencies

Solution: 

# Clean and reinstall
pip uninstall nexa -y
pip cache purge
pip install -e . --no-cache-dir

docs.title: 1.4 nexa: command not found
docs.text:
Symptom: 

$ nexa run hello.nx
bash: nexa: command not found

Reason: pip installation path is not in PATH, or virtual environment is not activated.

Solution: 

# Option 1: Ensure virtual environment is activated
source nexa-env/bin/activate

# Option 2: Use python -m to invoke
python -m nexa run hello.nx

# Option 3: Add pip path to PATH (not recommended)
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

docs.title: 2.1 Parse Error: Unexpected token
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ParseError: Unexpected token '}' at line 15

Common Causes:

  1. Missing comma or semicolon 

    // ❌ Wrong
agent Bot {
    role: "Assistant"    // Missing comma
    prompt: "..."
}

// ✅ Correct
agent Bot {
    role: "Assistant",
    prompt: "..."
}

  2. Mismatched brackets 

    // ❌ Wrong
flow main {
    result = Bot.run("hello"
}

// ✅ Correct
flow main {
    result = Bot.run("hello");
}

  3. Unclosed string 

    // ❌ Wrong
agent Bot {
    prompt: "This is a very long prompt
            with line break but not closed"
}

// ✅ Correct: Use triple quotes
agent Bot {
    prompt: """
        This is a very long prompt
        line break is fine
    """
}

docs.title: 2.2 Agent Undefined
docs.text:
Symptom: 

NameError: name 'MyAgent' is not defined

Reason: Agent is defined after flow, or typo.

Solution: 

// ❌ Wrong: Agent defined after flow
flow main {
    result = MyAgent.run("hello");
}

agent MyAgent {
    prompt: "..."
}

// ✅ Correct: Agent defined before flow
agent MyAgent {
    prompt: "..."
}

flow main {
    result = MyAgent.run("hello");
}

docs.title: 2.3 Property Name Typo
docs.text:
Symptom: Agent behaves abnormally, property not taking effect.

Common Typos: 

// ❌ Common misspellings
agent Bot {
    promt: "...",        // Should be prompt
    moedl: "gpt-4",      // Should be model
    rol: "Assistant"     // Should be role
}

// ✅ Correct spelling
agent Bot {
    prompt: "...",
    model: "gpt-4",
    role: "Assistant"
}

Checklist: | Correct Spelling | Common Errors | |---------|---------| | prompt | promt, prompts | | model | moedl, Model | | role | rol, Role | | tools | tool, Tool | | memory | memmory, Memory |

docs.title: 2.4 Protocol Syntax Error
docs.text:
Symptom: 

InvalidProtocolError: Protocol field type must be a string

Error Example: 

// ❌ Wrong: Type not wrapped in quotes
protocol UserInfo {
    name: string,        // Should be "string"
    age: int             // Should be "int"
}

// ✅ Correct
protocol UserInfo {
    name: "string",
    age: "int"
}

docs.title: 3.1 API Key Not Found
docs.text:
Symptom: 

RuntimeError: API key not found for provider 'openai'

Solution:

  1. Check if secrets.nxs file exists 

    ls -la secrets.nxs

  2. Check if key format is correct 

    # secrets.nxs content example
OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
DEEPSEEK_API_KEY = "sk-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
MINIMAX_API_KEY = "xxxxxxxxxxxx"
MINIMAX_GROUP_ID = "xxxxxxxxxxxx"

  3. Check file location 

    project/
├── secrets.nxs      # Must be in project root
├── main.nx
└── ...

docs.title: 3.2 Network Connection Timeout
docs.text:
Symptom: 

httpx.ConnectTimeout: Connection timed out

Solution:

  1. Check network connection 

    curl -I https://api.openai.com

  2. Configure proxy (if needed) 

    export HTTP_PROXY="http://proxy:port"
export HTTPS_PROXY="http://proxy:port"

  3. Increase timeout (in code) 

    agent Bot {
    model: "openai/gpt-4",
    prompt: "...",
    timeout: 60  // 60 second timeout
}

docs.title: 3.3 Out of Memory
docs.text:
Symptom: 

MemoryError: Unable to allocate array

Reason: Long conversation history or large number of concurrent Agents.

Solution:

  1. Enable context compression 

    agent Bot {
    prompt: "...",
    max_history_turns: 5  // Limit history turns
}

  2. Use cache to reduce repeated computation 

    agent Bot {
    prompt: "...",
    cache: true  // Enable intelligent cache
}

  3. Reduce concurrency 

    // ❌ Avoid too many parallel Agents
input |>> [A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, A6, A7, A8, A9, A10]

// ✅ Batch processing
input |>> [A1, A2, A3]

docs.title: 3.4 Loop Not Terminating
docs.text:
Symptom: loop until keeps running until timeout.

Reason: Termination condition too vague, LLM cannot accurately judge.

Solution:

  1. Use clearer termination conditions 

    // ❌ Vague condition
loop {
    draft = Writer.run(feedback);
    feedback = Reviewer.run(draft);
} until ("Article is perfect")  // Too subjective

// ✅ Clearer condition
loop {
    draft = Writer.run(feedback);
    feedback = Reviewer.run(draft);
} until ("Reviewer explicitly says 'passed' with no modification suggestions")

  2. Add maximum loop count protection 

    loop {
    draft = Writer.run(feedback);
    feedback = Reviewer.run(draft);
} until ("Article is perfect" or runtime.meta.loop_count >= 5)

// Check if exceeded
if (runtime.meta.loop_count >= 5) {
    print("Reached maximum retry count, please check manually");
}

docs.title: 4.1 Model Name Format Error
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ModelError: Unknown model format: gpt-4

Reason: Model name must include provider prefix.

Correct Format: 

// ✅ Correct format
model: "openai/gpt-4"
model: "openai/gpt-4-turbo"
model: "deepseek/deepseek-chat"
model: "minimax/minimax-m2.5"
model: "anthropic/claude-3-sonnet"

// ❌ Wrong format
model: "gpt-4"           // Missing provider
model: "GPT-4"           // Case error
model: "deepseek-chat"   // Missing slash

docs.title: 4.2 Model Does Not Support Certain Features
docs.text:
Symptom: 

NotImplementedError: Model 'xxx' does not support function calling

Solution:

Choose models that support required features:

Feature Supported Models Function Calling GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo, DeepSeek-Chat Structured Output GPT-4, Claude-3, DeepSeek-Chat Vision GPT-4-vision, Claude-3, MiniMax-VL

docs.title: 4.3 Rate Limit
docs.text:
Symptom: 

RateLimitError: Rate limit exceeded for model

Solution:

  1. Configure Fallback model 

    agent Bot {
    model: ["openai/gpt-4", fallback: "deepseek/deepseek-chat"],
    prompt: "..."
}

  2. Add retry configuration 

    agent Bot {
    model: "openai/gpt-4",
    prompt: "...",
    retry: 3,           // Retry count
    retry_delay: 5      // Retry delay (seconds)
}

  3. Use cache to reduce requests 

    agent Bot {
    cache: true,  // Reuse results for same requests
    prompt: "..."
}

docs.title: 4.4 Output Truncation
docs.text:
Symptom: Model output is truncated in the middle.

Reason: Reached token limit.

Solution:

agent Bot {
    prompt: "...",
    max_tokens: 4096  // Increase output length limit
}

Or use decorator: 

@limit(max_tokens=4096)
agent Bot {
    prompt: "..."
}

docs.title: 5.1 Tool Not Found
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ToolNotFoundError: Tool 'my_tool' not found in registry

Reason: Tool not properly registered or imported.

Solution:

  1. Check uses declaration 

    // ❌ Wrong: Tool not declared
agent Bot {
    prompt: "..."
}
// Later calling Bot.run() cannot use tools

// ✅ Correct: Declare tools to use
agent Bot uses std.http, std.fs {
    prompt: "..."
}

  2. Check standard library import 

    // If using custom tool, ensure path is correct
agent Bot uses "my_tools.py" {
    prompt: "..."
}

docs.title: 5.2 Tool Parameter Error
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ToolExecutionError: Invalid parameters for tool 'xxx'

Solution:

Check tool definition parameter format: 

// ❌ Wrong: Incorrect parameter format
tool MyTool {
    description: "Tool description",
    parameters: {
        param1: string  // Missing quotes
    }
}

// ✅ Correct
tool MyTool {
    description: "Tool description",
    parameters: {
        "param1": "string",
        "param2": "number"
    }
}

docs.title: 5.3 Tool Execution Timeout
docs.text:
Symptom: 

TimeoutError: Tool execution timed out after 30s

Solution:

agent Bot uses std.http {
    prompt: "...",
    tool_timeout: 60  // Increase timeout
}

docs.title: 6.1 Output Format Does Not Match Protocol
docs.text:
Symptom: 

ProtocolValidationError: Expected field 'xxx' but got 'yyy'

Reason: LLM output does not match defined Protocol.

Auto-fix Mechanism:

Nexa automatically attempts to fix, but if it fails multiple times, you can:

  1. Simplify Protocol 

    // ❌ Overly complex Protocol
protocol ComplexData {
    field1: "string",
    field2: "list[dict[string, any]]",  // Too complex
    field3: "dict[string, list[int]]"
}

// ✅ Simplified Protocol
protocol SimpleData {
    field1: "string",
    field2: "string",  // Use string to represent complex structure
    field3: "string"
}

  2. Specify format requirements in Prompt 

    agent DataExtractor implements MyProtocol {
    prompt: """
    Extract data and strictly output in JSON format.
    Must include fields: field1, field2, field3
    """
}

docs.title: 6.2 Protocol Type Mismatch
docs.text:
Symptom: 

TypeError: Expected 'int' but got 'string'

Solution:

Ensure types in Protocol match expected: 

// Correct type annotations
protocol DataTypes {
    text: "string",      // String
    number: "int",       // Integer
    price: "float",      // Float
    flag: "boolean",     // Boolean
    tags: "list[string]" // String list
}

docs.title: 7.1 Use nexa build to View Generated Code
docs.text:
# Generate Python code for debugging
nexa build script.nx

# Will generate out_script.py
# You can directly run or inspect this file
python out_script.py

docs.title: 7.2 Enable Verbose Logging
docs.text:
# Enable debug mode at runtime
nexa run script.nx --debug

# Or set environment variable
export NEXA_DEBUG=1
nexa run script.nx

docs.title: 7.3 Check Intermediate Results
docs.text:
Use print in flow to output intermediate results: 

flow main {
    step1 = Agent1.run(input);
    print("Step 1 result: " + step1);

    step2 = Agent2.run(step1);
    print("Step 2 result: " + step2);
}

docs.title: 7.4 Debug with Python SDK
docs.text:
from src.nexa_sdk import NexaRuntime

# Create runtime
runtime = NexaRuntime(debug=True)

# Run script
result = runtime.run_script("script.nx")

# Check result
print(result)

docs.title: 7.5 Check Cache Status
docs.text:
# View cache statistics
nexa cache stats

# Clear cache
nexa cache clear

docs.title: 8. Error Code Quick Reference
docs.text: Error Code Meaning Common Cause E001 Parse error Syntax error, mismatched brackets E002 Undefined identifier Agent/Tool undefined or typo E003 Type error Parameter type mismatch E101 API Key error Key not configured or invalid E102 Network error Connection timeout, proxy issue E103 Rate Limit Request frequency exceeded E201 Tool not found uses not declared or path error E202 Tool execution error Parameter error, timeout E301 Protocol validation failed Output format doesn't match definition E302 Protocol type error Field type mismatch

docs.title: 9. Getting Help
docs.text:
If the above solutions don't resolve your issue:

  1. Check Documentation: Complete Example Collection may have similar scenarios
  2. Submit Issue: Submit an Issue on GitHub repository, including:
  3. Complete error message
  4. Your code (sanitized)
  5. Runtime environment information
  6. Community Discussion: Use Giscus at the bottom of the documentation page to participate in discussions

📖 Don't panic when encountering problems, check this guide or seek community help!
